name: Artifact edge cases — ambiguous queries and grounding under format pressure

setup.description: Two papers (TOPCAT and Aldo-DHF) with conflicting results on spironolactone in HFpEF. Tests that artifact generation handles contradictory evidence properly — study guides must present the conflict honestly, FAQs must not fabricate consensus, and the system must not let format demands override grounding rules.

papers.title: TOPCAT: Treatment of Preserved Cardiac Function Heart Failure with an Aldosterone Antagonist
Aldo-DHF: Effect of Spironolactone on Diastolic Function in Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction

mockChunks.text: Spironolactone did NOT significantly reduce the primary composite endpoint of cardiovascular death, aborted cardiac arrest, or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.89; 95% CI 0.77-1.04; P=0.14).
3445 patients aged ≥50 years with symptomatic heart failure and ejection fraction ≥45% were enrolled. The median follow-up was 3.3 years.
A post-hoc regional analysis revealed significant heterogeneity: patients in the Americas showed benefit (HR 0.82; P=0.026), while patients in Russia/Georgia showed no benefit (HR 1.10; P=0.46).

Spironolactone significantly improved diastolic function as measured by E/e' ratio (change: -1.5 vs -0.1; P<0.001). However, spironolactone did NOT improve symptoms, exercise capacity, or quality of life.
422 patients with diastolic dysfunction and preserved ejection fraction (LVEF ≥50%) were enrolled. Patients received spironolactone 25mg daily for 12 months.

queries.query: Create a study guide about spironolactone in heart failure with preserved ejection fraction
expectedBehavior: Contains Main Findings from both TOPCAT and Aldo-DHF
TOPCAT negative result clearly stated with P=0.14
Aldo-DHF dissociation between function and symptoms noted
Contains Review Questions answerable from sources
Conflict between trials is honestly presented
failurePatterns: Claims spironolactone is effective in HFpEF
Omits the negative P=0.14 from TOPCAT

queries.query: Generate a FAQ about these spironolactone trials
expectedBehavior: Contains Q&A pairs with cited answers
Addresses the conflicting results between trials
TOPCAT negative P=0.14 mentioned in an answer
Aldo-DHF symptom vs function dissociation mentioned
Both trials covered in Q&A pairs

qualityCriteria: Study guide presents the conflicting evidence honestly
TOPCAT negative result (P=0.14) is prominently noted, not buried
Aldo-DHF dissociation (improved function, no symptom benefit) is clearly stated
No fabricated consensus — the evidence is genuinely mixed
Every factual claim has a citation [N]
FAQ does not imply spironolactone is effective when the evidence is equivocal
Regional analysis from TOPCAT is mentioned but appropriately caveated as post-hoc
